data.title: Trump sends copper prices crashing after his tariff exemption wrecked this popular trade
data.description:
By Myra P. Saefong

Comex copper prices drop by more than 20% Thursday

President Donald Trump's decision this week to impose tariffs starting Aug. 1 on imports of certain copper products, but not on refined copper itself, tanked prices for the industrial metal on Thursday - and has likely brought a halt to a popular trading strategy.

Arbitrage is a strategy that takes advantage of a difference in price for the same product in two or more markets. In copper's case, there's been a wide price spread between the Comex in the U.S. and London Metal Exchange in the U.K., so traders could buy cheaper copper on the LME and sell that for a higher price on the Comex to make a profit.

The arbitrage trade had "disrupted global copper trade as the metal was being rerouted to the U.S. to get ahead of the anticipated tariffs," said Steve Schoffstall, director of ETF product management at Sprott - and that trade "collapsed" after the announcement by the White House.

The White House on Wednesday afternoon released details on Trump's actions to address the threat copper imports pose to national security. The government will impose universal 50% tariffs on imports of semifinished copper products, such as copper pipes and wires, and copper-intensive derivative products, such as pipe fittings and cables, starting Aug. 1.

Copper input materials, such as copper ores, concentrates, mattes, cathodes, anodes and copper scrap, however, are not subject to these tariffs or reciprocal tariffs, according to the government fact sheet.

"For months, copper prices have been dislocated, with prices in the U.S. commanding significant premiums over LME-traded copper," Schoffstall told MarketWatch Thursday. That led to copper supply being directed to the U.S. ahead of the tariffs, with U.S. inventories reaching their highest levels in the last 21 years, while LME copper inventories fell by about 50% year to date, he said.

But Comex copper prices (HG00) (HGU25) suffered the biggest one-day plunge on record Thursday, losing nearly 22.1% from Wednesday's settlement to end at $4.3545 a pound. Prices had taken a dive late Wednesday afternoon as soon as details of the tariffs were released. The latest three-month closing price posted on the LME, which is delayed by a day, was $9,698.50 per metric tons, which is about $4.399 a pound. The plunge in Comex prices has narrowed the price spread between the two exchanges.

"Until the announcement, copper prices in the U.S. had been trading at a 28% premium over the LME prices, with the market front-running the expected tariff," Ewa Manthey, commodities strategist at ING, wrote in Wednesday note.

'Until the announcement, copper prices in the U.S. had been trading at a 28% premium over the LME prices, with the market front-running the expected tariff.'Ewa Manthey, ING

Trump's first musings of a tariff on copper imports back in January "unleased record shipments of the metal to American ports," she said. U.S. refined copper imports increased by almost 130% year over year from January to May, she said.

With much of the deliverable copper metals falling under the announced tariff exemptions, the "rapid physical arbitrage trade" that led to significant Comex warehouse copper inventories is "unwinding," said Neil Welsh, head of metals at brokerage Britannia Global Markets.

Back in February, Trump had ordered the start of a U.S. investigation into the potential threat that imports of copper pose to national security and wanted recommendations on ways to mitigate any such threat.

The president then announced on July 8 that he planned to place a 50% tariff on copper imports, leading to a 13% spike in copper prices that day, which lifted them to a record-high finish at the time of $5.6855 a pound. Prices reached a fresh record high on July 23 at $5.82.

Read: Trump's 50% tariff on copper imports is set to start Friday. Here's what could happen.

With details on the tariffs on imports more clear, copper prices should now move beyond the recent focus on those tariffs that had dominated sentiment for most of the year, Manthey said.

Copper prices have been rising this year "largely because the market has been front-running the tariff policy, and not because the demand picture has been improving or because the supply has actually been tightening," she said.

Natalie Scott-Gray, senior metals analyst at StoneX, said the focus for the copper market will shift to "global fundamentals," with the copper market in a supply surplus and a delayed recovery in cyclical demand this year.

The health of China is a "question mark," with expectations for slower growth in the second half of the year, and the European economy "struggling," she said.

-Myra P. Saefong

This content was created by MarketWatch, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. MarketWatch is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Cotton Hits Four-Week Low Amid Strong Dollar and Weak Demand
data.description:
Cotton futures dropped to 65.5 cents per pound on Thursday, hitting a four-week low as weakness in broader grain markets and a stronger dollar pressured prices.

The market lost momentum, with the resurgent dollar adding to the downside by making U.S. cotton less competitive for foreign buyers.

The dollar index extended its winning streak to a fifth session, rising 0.6% against a basket of major currencies.

Traders turned their focus to the upcoming USDA weekly export sales report, with expectations ranging between 100,000 and 150,000 running bales for both old and new crop.

Sluggish global demand and ongoing softness in agricultural commodities added to the bearish tone.

Amid the uncertainty, market participants remained cautious, awaiting fresh data to gauge the outlook for cotton demand.

data.title: LINCOLN NATIONAL CORP SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Lincoln National Corporation, a prominent player in the insurance and retirement solutions industry, has released its Form 10-Q report for the third quarter. The report provides a detailed overview of the company's financial performance and operational highlights, reflecting the challenges and strategic initiatives undertaken during the period.

Financial Highlights

Lincoln National Corporation reported the following key financial metrics for the third quarter:

  • Total Revenues: $8,735 million, reflecting a decrease from the previous period due to lower realized gains and other revenues.
  • Net Income (Loss): $(23) million, a significant decline compared to the previous period, primarily due to changes in market risk benefits and realized losses.
  • Net Income (Loss) Available to Common Stockholders: $(69) million, impacted by preferred stock dividends and the overall net loss.
  • Basic Net Income (Loss) Per Common Share: $(0.39), indicating a decrease in earnings per share compared to the previous period.
  • Diluted Net Income (Loss) Per Common Share: $(0.39), consistent with the basic earnings per share, reflecting the overall net loss.

Business Highlights

The company's business performance during the third quarter is summarized as follows:

  • Revenue Segments: Lincoln National Corporation operates through four business segments: Annuities, Life Insurance, Group Protection, and Retirement Plan Services. Each segment contributes to the company's overall revenue by offering a range of products such as variable annuities, fixed annuities, universal life insurance, and employer-sponsored retirement plans.
  • Geographical Performance: The company's operations are primarily within the United States, focusing on domestic markets for its insurance and retirement products.
  • Future Outlook: Management anticipates continued growth in the Annuities and Life Insurance segments, driven by demand for retirement and wealth protection products. The company is also focused on enhancing its digital capabilities to improve customer experience and operational efficiency.

SEC Filing: LINCOLN NATIONAL CORP [ LNC ] - 10-Q - Jul. 31, 2025

data.title: HF Sinclair Corp SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
HF Sinclair Corp, a leading energy company specializing in refining, renewables, and marketing, has released its Form 10-Q report for the second quarter of 2025. The report highlights the company's financial performance and operational achievements, reflecting its strategic initiatives and market conditions.

Financial Highlights

  • Sales and other revenues: $6,784 million, a decrease of 14% from the same period last year due to lower sales volumes and prices.
  • Income from operations: $275 million, an increase of 40% from the same period last year, reflecting improved refining margins and cost management.
  • Net income attributable to HF Sinclair stockholders: $208 million, an increase of 37% from the same period last year, driven by higher operating income and lower interest expenses.
  • Basic earnings per share: $1.10, an increase of 39% from the same period last year, reflecting higher net income.
  • Diluted earnings per share: $1.10, an increase of 39% from the same period last year, consistent with basic earnings per share.

Business Highlights

  • Refining Segment Performance: The Refining segment experienced improved refining margins leading into the summer driving season, particularly in the Mid-Continent and West regions. The completion of planned turnarounds at the Tulsa and Parco refineries also impacted results positively. For the third quarter of 2025, the company expects to process between 615,000-645,000 barrels per day of crude oil, considering the planned turnaround at the Puget Sound refinery.
  • Renewables Segment Performance: The Renewables segment saw improved margins, partly due to higher RINs prices in the second quarter of 2025. The company began recognizing partial benefits from the Producer’s Tax Credit (PTC) and anticipates capturing additional incremental value from the PTC in the third quarter of 2025.
  • Marketing Segment Performance: The Marketing segment continued to benefit from strong value in Sinclair branded sites, providing a consistent sales channel with margin uplift for produced fuels. The company aims to grow the number of branded sites by approximately 10% annually and added 55 net new branded sites during the three months ended June 30, 2025.
  • Lubricants & Specialties Segment Performance: The Lubricants & Specialties segment maintained solid performance, driven by sales mix optimization and base oil integration. Increased sales volumes were noted compared to the previous quarter, and the completion of a planned turnaround at the Mississauga facility also contributed to results.
  • Midstream Segment Performance: The Midstream segment benefited from higher pipeline revenues and lower operating expenses, although these were partially offset by lower throughput volumes during the three months ended June 30, 2025.
  • Geographical Performance: The company markets its refined products primarily in the Southwest United States, the Rocky Mountains extending into the Pacific Northwest, and other neighboring Plains states. The highest concentration of branded sites is located in the West and Mid-Continent regions.
  • Future Outlook: The company continues to adjust operational plans to evolving market conditions and expects to capture additional value from the Producer’s Tax Credit in the Renewables segment. The company also plans to expand its branded sites by approximately 10% annually.

SEC Filing: HF Sinclair Corp [ DINO ] - 10-Q - Jul. 31, 2025

data.title: COMSTOCK RESOURCES INC SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Comstock Resources Inc., a leading natural gas producer, has released its Form 10-Q report for the second quarter of 2025. The report highlights significant improvements in both financial and operational performance, driven by higher natural gas prices and effective cost management strategies.

Financial Highlights

  • Total Revenues: $470.3 million, increased by 90.5% compared to the same period in 2024 due to higher natural gas prices.
  • Operating Income (Loss): $89.4 million, compared to a loss of $92.5 million in the second quarter of 2024, reflecting improved pricing and cost management.
  • Net Income (Loss) Available to the Company: $124.8 million, compared to a loss of $126.3 million in the second quarter of 2024, driven by gains from derivative financial instruments.
  • Net Income (Loss) Per Share - Basic: $0.45, compared to a loss of $0.43 per share in the second quarter of 2024.
  • Net Income (Loss) Per Share - Diluted: $0.44, compared to a loss of $0.43 per share in the second quarter of 2024.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Segments: Natural gas sales increased significantly due to higher realized prices, with the average price per Mcf rising to $3.02 in Q2 2025 from $1.65 in Q2 2024. Oil sales, however, saw a decrease in volume, with only 13 MBbls sold in Q2 2025 compared to 15 MBbls in Q2 2024.
  • Sales Units: Natural gas production decreased by 14% to 112.2 Bcf in Q2 2025 from 130.9 Bcf in Q2 2024. Similarly, oil production decreased to 13 MBbls from 15 MBbls in the same period.
  • Gas Services Performance: Gas services revenue saw a substantial increase, rising by 346% to $130.3 million in Q2 2025 from $29.2 million in Q2 2024, driven by higher natural gas prices and increased sales of purchased natural gas.
  • Operational Costs: Production and ad valorem taxes decreased by 45% in Q2 2025 due to lower statutory tax rates in Louisiana and reduced production levels. Gathering and transportation costs also decreased by 15% due to lower production volumes.
  • Lease Operating Expenses: Lease operating expenses remained relatively stable at $31.1 million in Q2 2025 compared to $34.8 million in Q2 2024, despite a decrease in production volumes.
  • Future Outlook: The company plans to spend an additional $550 million to $650 million in the remaining six months of 2025 on drilling, completion, infrastructure, and other activities, indicating a continued focus on expanding operational capabilities.
  • Derivative Financial Instruments: The company reported net gains of $235.8 million from derivative financial instruments in Q2 2025, reflecting effective management of price risks associated with natural gas.
  • Capital Expenditures: The company drilled 19 wells and completed 24 wells in the Haynesville and Bossier shale during the first six months of 2025, highlighting ongoing investment in exploration and development activities.

SEC Filing: COMSTOCK RESOURCES INC [ CRK ] - 10-Q - Jul. 31, 2025

data.title: AMETEK INC/ SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
AMETEK INC/, a leading global manufacturer of electronic instruments and electromechanical devices, has released its Form 10-Q report for the second quarter of 2025. The report showcases the company's robust financial performance and strategic operational advancements, driven by acquisitions and favorable market conditions.

Financial Highlights

  • Net Sales: $1,778.1 million, an increase of 2.5% compared with the second quarter of 2024, driven by acquisitions and favorable foreign currency translation.
  • Cost of Sales: $1,142.2 million, representing 64.2% of net sales, a slight increase from 64.0% in the second quarter of 2024.
  • Operating Income: $461.6 million, an increase of 3.2% from the second quarter of 2024, with operating margins improving to 26.0% of net sales.
  • Net Income: $358.4 million, an increase of 6.1% compared with the second quarter of 2024, reflecting strong operational performance.
  • Diluted Earnings Per Share: $1.55, an increase of 6.9% from $1.45 in the second quarter of 2024, supported by higher net income and operational efficiencies.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Segments: The Electronic Instruments Group (EIG) reported net sales of $1,159.6 million for Q2 2025, a slight increase of 0.5% compared to Q2 2024. The Electromechanical Group (EMG) achieved record net sales of $618.5 million, marking a 6.4% increase over the same period last year.
  • Geographical Performance: International sales for Q2 2025 were $843.0 million, representing 47.4% of total net sales, up from 45.5% in Q2 2024. This growth was driven by increased demand across all markets and contributions from recent acquisitions.
  • Sales Units: Orders for Q2 2025 reached $1,782.1 million, a 6.3% increase from Q2 2024, with a notable 5% favorable effect from foreign currency translation.
  • Operational Excellence Initiatives: The company continued to benefit from its Operational Excellence initiatives, which contributed to improved operating margins despite the dilutive impact of recent acquisitions and foreign currency headwinds.
  • Future Outlook: The company is actively monitoring the impact of tariffs and trade restrictions, implementing strategic adjustments to its global supply chains, and leveraging its worldwide manufacturing footprint to mitigate potential adverse effects on demand, costs, and the global economy.

SEC Filing: AMETEK INC/ [ AME ] - 10-Q - Jul. 31, 2025

data.title: Equities ease from earlier highs with Amazon, Apple earnings due
data.description:
Key points:
  • Microsoft, Meta rise after blowout quarterly results
  • Semiconductor stocks weak
  • Economic data shows inflation rise, stable labor market
  • Indexes: Dow off 0.29%, S&P 500 up 0.01%, Nasdaq 0.27%

U.S. stocks were well off earlier highs on Thursday, as the Nasdaq and S&P clung to slight gains following the latest swath of corporate earnings and economic data, with results from megacaps Amazon and Apple due after the closing bell.

Microsoft posted a strong earnings report and briefly surpassed the $4 trillion market cap threshold , becoming only the second publicly traded company to ever touch the milestone after Nvidia . Its shares were last up 3.9% after climbing as much as 8.2% on the session.

Meta Platforms jumped 11.9% after hitting a record intraday high of $784.75 as AI-driven growth in its core ad business powered a bullish revenue forecast.

Still, other AI-related names such as chipmakers Broadcom and Nvidia were weaker on the session, pulling the PHLX semiconductor index down more than 3%.

"Looking at the market action today, you have haves and have-nots, and so you have a couple tech companies, like a lot of the semiconductor-related and semi-cap equipment-related stocks are doing pretty poorly," said Ellen Hazen, chief market strategist at F.L. Putnam Investment Management in Lynnfield, Massachusetts.

"But then, of course, Microsoft is doing pretty well, and the same thing with Amazon and Meta, which are doing really well."

The Dow Jones Industrial Average fell 130.37 points, or 0.29%, to 44,330.91, the S&P 500 gained 0.38 points, or 0.01%, to 6,363.28 and the Nasdaq Composite gained 56.76 points, or 0.27%, to 21,186.44.

The S&P 500 had risen as much as 1% and the Nasdaq as much as 1.5% earlier in the session. The Nasdaq has not logged a move of at least 1% in either direction since July 3 while the S&P last recorded a daily 1% move on June 24.

Earlier economic data from the Commerce Department report showed inflation picked up in June, with new tariffs pushing prices higher and stoking expectations that price pressures could intensify in the coming months, while weekly initial jobless claims signaled the labor market remained on stable footing.

Investors will now eye Friday's non-farm payrolls report and a looming tariff deadline, as U.S. President Donald Trump was expected to issue higher final duty rates for countries that have not reached an agreement, although Mexico was granted a 90-day reprieve.

U.S. stocks have rallied after a sharp selloff that began in early April after Trump announced a bevy of sharp tariffs, only to rebound as deals have been struck with many trading partners on duty levels. The Dow, S&P 500 and Nasdaq are each on track for their third straight monthly gain.

Drug stocks were also weaker after the White House said Trump sent letters to the CEOs of 17 major pharmaceutical companies, urging immediate action to lower the cost of prescription drugs for Americans. The NYSE Arca pharmaceutical index (.DRG) was down 2.3%.

Declining issues outnumbered advancers by a 1.38-to-1 ratio on the NYSE, and by a 1.71-to-1 ratio on the Nasdaq.

The S&P 500 posted 33 new 52-week highs and 25 new lows while the Nasdaq Composite recorded 65 new highs and 126 new lows.

data.title: PREVIEW: Amazon shares climb; seen reporting y/y growth in revenue and EPS
data.description:
** Shares in Amazon.com up ~2% on Thursday ahead of quarterly report due out after the market close with investor anticipation high after impressive reports from Microsoft and Meta Platforms late on Wednesday

** Wall Street sees AMZN reporting Q2 adj EPS of $1.33 vs $1.26 in Q2 2024 on rev of $162.09 bln, which would be up 9.5% vs $147.98 bln year ago, according to LSEG data

** In last 8 quarters AMZN's EPS beat the Street very handily every time while revenue met or beat expectations 7 times with a ~0.4% miss in Q2 2024

** Amazon is expected to seek to reassure investors that Amazon Web Services (AWS), its cloud business, is growing fast enough to offset any pullback in consumer spending that could hurt its retail operations. AWS, which accounts for less than a fifth of AMZN sales but typically ~60% of its profit, is expected to have grown ~17% in the April-June period

** In busier than average trading session AMZN shares are ~$235 vs median PT of $250, which is above $240 median a month ago, according to LSEG, which shows 73 analyst ratings: 22 'strong buy,' 47 'buy' and 4 'hold' ratings

** YTD it has risen ~7% vs gain of ~5% for the blue chip Dow Industrial Average and a 0.9% decline for the S&P 500 consumer discretionary index

** ** AMZN options primed for a 5.8% swing for the shares, in either direction, on Fri; over the last eight quarters, on average, the shares moved 5.5% the day after results, according to Trade Alert

data.title: Copper Prices Fall 22% in Record Daily Drop — WSJ
data.description:
By Ryan Dezember

U.S. copper futures ended Thursday down more than 22%, by far their worst single-day drop on record, as traders reversed course following President Trump's tariff announcement late Wednesday.

Rather than imposing a 50% tariff on raw copper, as the market had been expecting since Trump's threat in early July to do so, the White House said the 50% tariff would be collected on imported copper products, like wire and pipe. Concentrate and ore from mines, scrap and less processed forms of the industrial metal, including cathodes, were excluded from import tax that takes effect on Friday.

U.S. copper prices surged to records and well above global prices earlier this month when Trump said he would slap a 50% import tax on copper. When the White House said less-processed copper would be exempt from tariffs, futures plunged and profits evaporated for many on Wall Street.

The slow-to-shift commodity trading computers programmed to follow long-term price trends were caught pointed the wrong way.

"We basically just gave up all the P&L that we had made on the tariff in about a minute and a half," said David Aspell, co-chief investment officer at Mount Lucas Management, which runs a big trend-following fund. "It's gone from a pretty good winner into a small loser, which is not disastrous but it's not great."

The firm's actively managed fund also trades copper futures but got out before Wednesday's crash. The fund's managers locked in gains when the rise in U.S. futures stalled out around a 25% premium to the global price set in London's trading pits. That is where the firm expected the tariff rate to settle once certain exporting countries negotiated for exemptions and lower rates, Aspell said.

He said he expects a few more days of turmoil while wrong way trades are unwound, and is looking to bet anew on rising copper prices.

"You still need more copper and it's not going to be incentivized with prices here," Aspell said. "It should grind its way back up as it trades on its own fundamentals rather than implied tariff rates."

This item is part of a Wall Street Journal live coverage event. The full stream can be found by searching P/WSJL (WSJ Live Coverage).

data.title: Chevron Is On a Roll Ahead of Second-Quarter Earnings — Barrons.com
data.description:
Avi Salzman

Chevron has been on a roll as it gets set to report second-quarter earnings on Friday. The company just closed its acquisition of oil producer Hess, and got permission from the Trump administration to resume operations in Venezuela.

Those developments won't show up in second-quarter numbers, which are likely to be relatively downbeat. Analysts expect Chevron to post earnings per share of $1.73, according to FactSet, versus $2.55 a year ago. Oil prices have fallen from last year's level, and the company has slowed production growth. Shares are up 4% this year, versus a rise of 8% for the S&P 500.

But fundamentally, Chevron is in the best shape it has been in years. Finalizing the Hess deal was no easy feat. It had been in limbo for nearly two years, because Exxon Mobil had said that it should have a right to buy Hess' stake in a major oil project in Guyana that Exxon operates. Chevron won an arbitration case over that issue just a few weeks ago, and completed the acquisition.

Hess' stake in the Guyana project fills a void for Chevron. Investors had been worried that Chevron does not have enough growth opportunities around the world, compared with Exxon. The Guyana project is expected to produce hundreds of thousands of barrels' worth of oil every day for the rest of the decade and beyond.

Among the big questions for Chevron now is what the company will do with its stock-buybacks after announcing plans last quarter to reduce their pace. Any sign that buybacks are ramping up again should help the stock.

Write to Avi Salzman at avi.salzman@barrons.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Cells: Hemostemix ACP-01 Provides the Scientific Basis for Improving the Longevity and Signal Uptake of Brain Computer Implants
data.description:
Calgary, Alberta--(Newsfile Corp. - July 31, 2025) - Hemostemix (OTCQB: HMTXF) (FSE: 2VF0) ("Hemostemix" or the "Company") is excited to highlight a groundbreaking research article published in Cells on June 29, 2025, by Fraser C. Henderson Sr. and Ms. Kelly Tuchman, exploring how a combination of the patient's own ACP-01 and NCP-01 (autologous blood-derived cell precursors) may support the long-term performance of brain-computer interfaces (BCIs).

Key Scientific Insights

The Challenge with BCIs: Inflammation, scarring, and programmed cell death undermine performance over time

Implantable electrodes within the brain have enabled remarkable achievements—e.g., paralyzed individuals playing chess and blind individuals recognizing letters. However, these devices often fail between six months and one year. The longest BCI in use lasted seven years. Even then, issues like inflammation, scarring, and programmed cell death (apoptosis) undermine performance over time.

Hemostemix's Innovative Cell-Based SolutionThe article proposes using two types of autologous (patient-derived) progenitor cells: Angiogenic Cell Precursors (ACP-01, VesCell™) and Neural Cell Precursors (NCP-01), delivered into the cerebrospinal fluid, to foster a healing cellular environment around the implant.

  • ACP-01 (VesCell™) produces signals like IL-8, VEGF, and angiogenin. They attract natural killer (NK) cells that suppress inflammation, reduce tissue scarring, and support new blood vessel growth (angiogenesis).

  • ACP-01potentiate healing through the expression of tissue regeneration factors CXCL8, VEGF, and angiogenin. They include CD34+ (blood stem cell). CXCL8 recruit endogenous CD34+, that circulate peripherally, to the site of implant.

  • CXCL8 (interleukin-8) activate NF-κB, resulting in gene transcription and protein synthesis necessary for learning (memory formation and consolidation).

  • NCP- 01express receptors like CXCR4, and migrate toward the BCI site. NCP help shift immune cells into a neuroprotective (M2) state. NCP differentiate into neurons and supporting glial cells that promote new synaptic connections and neural plasticity.

Figure 1. Natural Killer (NK) cells recruited by angiogenic cell precursors (ACPs) suppress inflammation through the release of anti-inflammatory cytokines, dendritic cell and monocyte maturation, and lysis of auto-aggressive T cells. Created in BioRender. Tuchman, K. (2025) https://biorender.com/6rfoaiv

To view an enhanced version of this graphic, please visit:

https://images.newsfilecorp.com/files/5065/260846_d1729e68f4832a43_001full.jpg

Figure 2. Angiogenic cell precursors (ACPs) potentiate healing through expression of tissue regeneration factors such as the chemokine interleukin-8 (CXCL8), vascular endothelial growth factor (VEGF) and angiogenin. In addition to the robust presence of CD34+ in ACPs, the expressed CXCL8 recruits peripheral CD34+ precursor cells, further supporting angiogenesis. Created by BioRender. Tuchman, K. (2025) https://biorender.com/w2cthsg

To view an enhanced version of this graphic, please visit:

https://images.newsfilecorp.com/files/5065/260846_d1729e68f4832a43_002full.jpg

Figure 3. Interleukin-8 (CXCL8) is expressed by angiogenic cell precursors (ACPs), and activates

the canonical NF-κB pathway, resulting in gene transcription and protein synthesis necessary for

memory formation and consolidation. Created in BioRender. Tuchman, K. (2025) https://BioRender.com/u2icrk5

To view an enhanced version of this graphic, please visit:

https://images.newsfilecorp.com/files/5065/260846_d1729e68f4832a43_003full.jpg

Mechanism of Action

Together, ACP and NCP support a cascade of beneficial molecular events:

  • Activation of neurotrophic factors and NF-κB pathways that protect cells (anti-apoptosis).

  • Promotion of synaptogenesis (formation of connections between neurons), neuritogenesis (growth of neural processes), to improve learning-related plasticity.

  • As the patient's DNA-based engineered cellular environment, ACP and NCP home to the site of BCI, decrease inflammation, increase angiogenesis, increase neuro-protectiveness, promote new synaptic connections, and may dramatically extend both the lifespan of BCI, signal fidelity and learning.

Why This Matters to Hemostemix

Hemostemix's patented proprietary platform produces ACP-01 (angiogenic precursors) and NCP-01 (neural precursors) from a patient's own blood. This new research provides a scientific basis of how the combination of ACP-01+NCP-01 promises to overcome the major limitations in BCI implants.

  • The findings align with Hemostemix's vision of licensing ACP-01 and NCP-01 to enhance BCI longevity and functionalities.

Forward-Looking Perspective

This research paves the way for:

  • Licensing with BCI technology companies to combine autologous stem-cell support of BCI with cutting-edge patient-based angiogenic and neural interfaces.

  • Rapid preclinical and clinical testing of ACP-01 + NCP-01 at BCI implant sites.

"We imagine a world where the environment for BCI, the patient's brain, augmented by the patients' own stem cells, improves the longevity of the implants from months to a lifetime, improves signal fidelity to increase functional movement, and increases learning and memory retention," stated Dr. Fraser Henderson, lead author.

"I want to thank Dr. Henderson and Ms. Tuchman for this very thorough exposition of the benefits of using one's own DNA structured as ACP & NCP to improve BCIs. Truly, this is incredible work. Coupled with our market analyses of the top three BCI companies, shareholders can expect more news to follow, as Mr. Lawrence, Chief Commercialization Officer and I meet with potential partners," stated Thomas Smeenk, CEO.

In Summary

Dr. Henderson has outlined a new cell-based approach that may allow brain implants to work reliably for years. By delivering two types of the patient's blood-derived stem cell precursors—one that fights inflammation and boosts blood flow, the other that helps build new neuronal cells—this method could protect and improve the brain at the site of BCI implant. Hemostemix's ready-to-use cell products (ACP-01 and NCP-01) are a direct fit for this approach and could potentially transform how long and well brain-computer interfaces function.

ABOUT HEMOSTEMIX

Founded in 2003, Hemostemix is a stem-cell therapeutics company with patented technology to generate autologous angiogenic and neural cell precursors from patient blood. Its ACP-01 and NCP-01 cell lines aim to treat cardiovascular, neurological, and implant-related conditions. A winner of the World Economic Forum Technology Pioneer Award, the Company has developed, patented, is scaling and selling autologous (patient's own) blood-based stem cell therapy, VesCell™ (ACP-01). Hemostemix has completed seven clinical studies of 318 subjects and published its results in 11 peer reviewed publications. ACP-01 is safe, clinically relevant and statistically significant as a treatment for peripheral arterial disease, chronic limb threatening ischemia, non ischemic dilated cardiomyopathy, ischemic cardiomyopathy, congestive heart failure, and angina. Hemostemix completed its Phase II clinical trial for chronic limb threatening ischemia and published its results in the Journal of Biomedical Research & Environmental Science. As compared to a five year mortality rate of 60% in the CLTI patient population, UBC and U of T reported to the 41st meeting of vascular surgeons: 0% mortality, cessation of pain, wound healing in 83% of patients followed for up to 4.5 years, as a midpoint result. For more information, please visit www.hemostemix.com.

For further information, please contact: Thomas Smeenk, President, CEO & Co-Founder: EM: tsmeenk@hemostemix.com / PH: 905-580-4170

Neither the TSX Venture Exchange nor its Regulation Service Provider (as that term is defined under the policies of the TSX Venture Exchange) accepts responsibility for the adequacy or accuracy of this release.

data.title: Corn, soybean basis steady to weaker at river terminals, processors, ethanol plants
data.description:
Basis bids for corn and soybeans were mostly steady to lower at elevators, processors and river terminals in a spot check of facilities in the U.S. Midwest on Thursday, with some farmers clearing out old crops and softer export demand, according to analysts.

  • The corn basis fell at processors in Cedar Rapids, Iowa and Lincoln, Nebraska, a river terminal in Savanna, Illinois and an elevator in Cincinnati, Ohio.

  • The soybean basis fell at processors in Decatur, Illinois and Morristown, Indiana as well as Cedar Rapids and Council Bluffs, Iowa.

  • "A lot of buyers now claim to have adequate coverage until harvest," said Karl Setzer, a partner at Consus Ag, though he said in some locations merchandisers were seeing little movement.

  • Setzer said processing margins were softer, weighing on the basis, and "the export market is not pushing as hard for inventory as interest shifts to new crop coverage, easing competition for what bushels are moving."

  • CBOT September corn futures (CU25) settled up 2-1/4 cents at $3.94 a bushel and new-crop December corn (CZ25) finished up 1-1/2 cents at $4.13-3/4 a bushel.

  • CBOT August soybeans (SQ25) ended down 6 cents at $9.61-3/4 a bushel and the new-crop November contract (SX25) was down 6-1/2 cents at $9.89-1/4.

data.title: Colombia's central bank holds interest rate at 9.25%
data.description:
Colombia's central bank held the benchmark interest rate at 9.25% on Thursday, surprising the majority of the market, which expected a rate cut of 25 basis points amid lower inflation and persistent fiscal concerns.

The decision was backed by a majority of the seven-member board.

data.title: Tesla starts ride-hailing service in the Bay Area. It's not a robotaxi yet.
data.description:
By Claudia Assis

Musk has predicted Tesla robotaxi rides for half the U.S. population by the end of the year

Tesla Inc. has started offering ride-hailing services in the San Francisco Bay Area, the birthplace of ride-sharing and where the EV maker has plenty of competition from services with drivers at the wheel as well as from self-driving cars.

In a post on X.com, the social-media platform owned by Tesla Chief Executive Elon Musk, Tesla (TSLA) said the invitations for the ride-hailing were going out for select few in the area, which includes the cities of San Francisco and San Jose.

It's not quite a robotaxi ride, as drivers are at the wheel. Tesla has not gathered all permits necessary to operate a true self-driving service in the state, according to reports.

Tesla launched a small-scale robotaxi program in Austin, Texas, last month. The Model Ys operating that service have safety riders in the passenger seat and roll along in circumscribed, or geofenced, areas of the city.

On a call last week with analysts after Tesla's second-quarter results, Musk hailed the "successful" Austin robotaxi launch and said that in the next few weeks Tesla will expand the service area to exceed what competitors are doing.

Tesla is waiting for regulatory approvals to launch the service in the San Francisco Bay Area, Arizona and Florida, Musk said.

And in a bold pronouncement, Musk said that the company hopes to be offering robotaxi services to roughly half the U.S. population by the end of the year.

The Bay Area is home to Uber Technologies Inc. (UBER) and Lyft Inc. (LYFT), which are among the pioneers in ride-hailing.

The region is also home turf to one of the heavyweights in driverless technology, Alphabet Inc.'s (GOOGL) (GOOG) Waymo.

Waymo recently completed more than 100 million driverless miles on public roads, from about 50 million driverless miles at the end of last year.

"Waymo remains the industry leader in autonomous driving, both in terms of vehicle fleet size, territory covered and autonomous miles driven with customers on board," Andres Sheppard at Cantor Fitzgerald said in a note earlier this year.

Waymo this week announced the start of services in Dallas, Texas. It also operates in Phoenix, Los Angeles, Austin and Atlanta, and has begun mapping routes in Washington, D.C. and New York City.

"Still, they will need to work on lowering their hardware costs as they continue to scale up, which remains the biggest question, in our view," Sheppard said.

Tesla's robotaxi service is "still very small scale," accumulating about 7,000 miles since the June 22 launch with some 10 to 20 vehicles on the road, Edison Yu at Deutsche Bank said in a note this week.

"Looking forward, we expect the fleet to grow to +1,000 vehicles by mid-2026 and then perhaps begin having some material contribution to financials in 2027," Yu said. "Further patience" from investors is required for robotaxis, he said.

In December, General Motors Co. (GM) scuppered its Cruise autonomous-vehicle business, citing the amount of time and resources needed to scale the business and an "increasingly competitive" market for AVs.

Cruise was under fire on several fronts - including having its license to offer robotaxi rides in California suspended after one of its AVs dragged a pedestrian who had been hit by a vehicle driven by a human driver in San Francisco in 2023.

-Claudia Assis

This content was created by MarketWatch, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. MarketWatch is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: LINCOLN ELECTRIC HOLDINGS INC SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Lincoln Electric Holdings Inc., a global leader in the manufacturing of welding products, has released its Form 10-Q report for the second quarter of 2025. The report highlights significant financial growth and operational achievements, driven by strategic pricing, acquisitions, and effective cost management.

Financial Highlights

  • Net sales: $1,088.7 million, increased by 6.6% compared to the same period in 2024, driven by higher pricing and acquisitions.
  • Gross profit: $405.5 million, representing 37.3% of sales, slightly down from 37.6% in the prior year due to lower volumes offset by higher operational efficiencies.
  • Operating income: $192.1 million, or 17.6% of sales, up 29.1% from the prior year period, reflecting improved cost management and reduced rationalization charges.
  • Net income: $143.4 million, increased by 41.0% from the prior year, benefiting from higher sales and improved operational efficiencies.
  • Diluted earnings per share: $2.56, up 44.6% from $1.77 in the prior year, reflecting strong earnings growth and effective cost management.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Segments: The company's net sales are divided into three main segments: Americas Welding, International Welding, and The Harris Products Group. The Americas Welding segment saw a 7.4% increase in net sales for the three months ended June 30, 2025, driven by price increases and acquisitions. The Harris Products Group experienced an 18.8% increase in net sales, attributed to higher organic sales and effective cost management.
  • Geographical Performance: The Americas Welding segment, which includes operations in North and South America, reported a 6.0% increase in net sales for the six months ended June 30, 2025. In contrast, the International Welding segment, covering Europe, Africa, Asia, and Australia, saw a 4.8% decrease in net sales, primarily due to lower volumes and the impact of foreign currency exchange rates.
  • Sales Units: The company reported a decrease in volumes for both the Americas Welding and International Welding segments, particularly in equipment sales, which impacted overall sales performance.
  • Future Outlook: The company is monitoring evolving trade negotiations and potential impacts from tariffs on imported goods into the U.S. It plans to continue addressing these challenges and is prepared to take additional measures if necessary. The company also anticipates that rationalization actions will positively impact future results of operations.

SEC Filing: LINCOLN ELECTRIC HOLDINGS INC [ LECO ] - 10-Q - Jul. 31, 2025

GLOBAL Wall Street outperformed European equities on Thursday, boosted by megacap earnings reports, even as data showed signs of rising inflation and investors watched for last-minute trade deals ahead of U.S. President Donald Trump's August 1 deadline.

MSCI's gauge of stocks across the globe EURONEXT:IACWI fell 1.07 points.

For a full report, click on

- - - -

NEW YORK - The S&P 500 and Nasdaq receded from record highs notched earlier on Thursday, as Wall Street shifted its focus beyond Microsoft's $4 trillion milestoneand braced for Apple and Amazon's earnings later in the day.

The Nasdaq Composite jumped as much as 1.2% in its strongest intraday rally in nearly a month, before paring gains to 0.8%, while the S&P 500 advanced 0.45%. The Dow Jones Industrial Average , meanwhile, slipped into negative territory and then became flat.

For a full report, click on

- - - -

LONDON - European shares closed at a more than one-week low on Thursday, as investors were disappointed by a series of corporate reports from the likes of Sanofi and Ferrari, while beverage makers slid as they were faced with a 15% U.S. tariff.

The pan-European STOXX 600 index finished 0.75% lower.

For a full report, click on

- - - -

TOKYO - Japanese stocks rose on Thursday while the yen shed early gains after the Bank of Japan flagged continuing risks to the economic outlook in keeping interest rates steady.

The Nikkei share average ended the day up 1% at 41,069.82.

For a full report, click on

- - - -

SHANGHAI - China stocks on Thursday posted their steepest one-day decline since April, as sluggish factory data and the Politburo's lack of stimulus signals weighed on sentiment.

At the market close, the Shanghai Composite Index .SSEC was down 1.2% to 3,573.21.

For a full report, click on

- - - -

AUSTRALIA - Australian shares closed slightly lower on Thursday, mainly dragged by heavyweight miners as Rio Tinto fell more than 3% after reporting its smallest first-half profit in five years.

The S&P ASX 200 benchmark index slipped 13.6 points, or 0.2%, to finish at 8,742.80. The index rose more than 2% in July, clocking its fourth consecutive month of gains.

For a full report, click on

- - - -

SEOUL - South Korean shares erased early gains to end lower on Thursday, as automakers dropped on the country's trade deal with the United States, outweighing a rally among shipbuilders.

The benchmark KOSPI closed down 9.03 points, or 0.28%, at 3,245.44, after rising as much as 1.04% in early trade to its highest level since August 2021.

For a full report, click on

- - - -

FOREIGN EXCHANGE

NEW YORK - The dollar was poised for its first monthly gain of the year against major currencies on Thursday, underpinned by easing trade tensions and U.S. economic resilience.

The dollar index was flat at 99.85 after rising nearly 1% in the previous session. It is on track for the first monthly gain in 2025.

For a full report, click on

- - - -

SHANGHAI - China's yuan rebounded from a near two-month low against the dollar on Thursday, bolstered by stronger-than-expected guidance from the central bank, signalling its commitment to currency stability.

The onshore yuan recovered from a near two-month trough of 7.2 hit overnight to trade at 7.1921 per dollar as of 0402 GMT.

For a full report, click on

- - - -

AUSTRALIA - The Australian and New Zealand dollars steadied on Thursday after losing ground to a broadly firmer U.S. currency, while the Aussie drew support from surprising strength in domestic consumption data.

The Aussie edged up 0.3% to $0.6451 , having slid 1.2% the previous session to a one-month low of $0.6427. That was the fifth straight session of losses and leaves the Aussie a long way from its recent eight-month top of $0.6625.

For a full report, click on

- - - -

SEOUL - South Korean shares erased early gains to end lower on Thursday, as automakers dropped on the country's trade deal with the United States, outweighing a rally among shipbuilders.

The most liquid three-year Korean treasury bond yield (KR3YT=RR) rose by 1.0 basis point to 2.465%.

For a full report, click on

- - - -

TREASURIES

NEW YORK - U.S. Treasury yields slid on Thursday in choppy trading, after rising the previous session following less dovish comments from Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell, as investors balanced positions ahead of another important indicator - nonfarm payrolls for July - on Friday.

In late morning trading, U.S. 10-year yields were last down 3.8 basis points (bps) at 4.340% , but up 11 bps on the month.

For a full report, click on

- - - -

LONDON - Money markets pared back their bets on European Central Bank rate cuts on Thursday, now pricing in just a 50% chance of an additional 25-basis-point easing move by the end of the year.

Germany's 10-year bond yield, the benchmark for the euro zone, dropped 2 bps to 2.69%.

For a full report, click on

- - - -

TOKYO - Japan's shorter-dated bonds pared declines on Thursday after the Bank of Japan policy statement caused market participants to push out expectations for any future interest rate hike.

The two-year JGB yield (JP2YTN=JBTC) rose to as high as 0.835% to hit the session high after the BOJ's decision, and was last at 0.825%, up 0.5 basis points.

For a full report, click on

COMMODITIES

GOLD - Gold prices rose on Thursday as traders turned to the safe-haven asset amid ongoing tariff uncertainty ahead of U.S. President Donald Trump's August 1 deadline to end negotiations.

Spot gold was up 0.6% at $3,294.56 per ounce, as of 01:54 p.m. ET (17:54 GMT), after rising more than 1% earlier today.

For a full report, click on

- - - -

IRON ORE - Iron ore futures prices declined for a second straight session on Thursday to hit their lowest levels in two weeks, as weaker-than-expected July factory activity data in top consumer China raised demand concerns.

The most-traded September iron ore contract on China's Dalian Commodity Exchange (DCE) closed daytime trade 2.38% lower at 779 yuan ($108.32) a metric ton, the lowest since July 17.

For a full report, click on

- - - -

BASE METALS - U.S. copper prices registered the biggest one-day decline on record on Thursday as investors scrambled to adjust positions after a surprise move by U.S. President Donald Trump to exclude widely traded refined metal from 50% import tariffs.

Benchmark three-month copper on the London Metal Exchange dropped 0.9% to $9,608 a ton.

For a full report, click on

- - - -

OIL - Oil prices declined on Thursday as investors considered the extension of an existing trade deal between the U.S. and Mexico, while a surprise build in U.S. crude stocks on Wednesday also dragged down prices.

Brent crude futures for September , set to expire on Thursday, declined by 74 cents, or 1.01%, to $72.50 a barrel by 1:31 p.m. EDT (1731 GMT).

For a full report, click on

- - - -

PALM OIL - Malaysian palm oil futures snapped two straight sessions of gains on Thursday, asweak rival edible oils weighed on prices, while concerns over rising production and sluggish export demand added further pressure.

The benchmark palm oil contract for October delivery on the Bursa Malaysia Derivatives Exchange slid 48 ringgit, or 1.12%, to 4,230 ringgit ($992.96) a metric ton at the close.

For a full report, click on

- - - -

RUBBER - Japanese rubber futures fell across all exchanges on Thursday as traders booked profits after last week's rally, while fresh U.S. tariffs on South Korean autos and weak manufacturing data weighed.

The Osaka Exchange (OSE) rubber contract for January delivery , was down 7.2 yen, or 2.23%, at 315.5 yen ($2.12) per kg.

For a full report, click on

- - - -

data.title: Waste Management Is Maintained at Sector Outperform by Scotiabank
HANetf and VettaFi Partner to Launch the World's First Indo-Pacific Defence ETF, Addressing Critical Gap in Global Portfolios
data.description:
New York, New York--(Newsfile Corp. - July 31, 2025) - HANetf, Europe's leading white-label UCITS ETF provider, announced the launch of the Future of Defence Indo-Pacific ex-China UCITS ETF (LSE: QUAD). The new ETF, which is based on an index provided by VettaFi, offers investors targeted exposure to defence, aerospace, and security companies in the Indo-Pacific region, excluding China.

The launch of QUAD comes as Indo-Pacific nations are undergoing a historic rearmament and accelerating the localization of military production, a trend that is severely underrepresented in existing global defence ETFs. VettaFi's Future of Defence Indo-Pac ex-China Index, which the new ETF tracks, is designed to capture this significant geopolitical shift.

"We are proud to partner with HANetf to bring this innovative and timely product to market," said Brian Coco, Head of Product at VettaFi. "As the strategic weight of global security continues to shift eastward, our index provides the precise, forward-looking exposure that investors are seeking. This ETF is a prime example of our commitment to delivering specialized benchmarks that address the most compelling market opportunities."

Hector McNeil, Co-Founder and Co-CEO of HANetf, noted that the launch of the QUAD ETF fills a critical gap in European portfolios by providing access to the defence buildup across key Indo-Pacific democracies. This launch cements HANetf's position as Europe's most comprehensive platform for accessing the global defence theme.

The VettaFi Future of Defence Indo-Pac ex-China Index rebalances quarterly to ensure a focused, diversified exposure to the sector. The new ETF is available with a total expense ratio of 0.59%.

About VettaFi: VettaFi is a differentiated index provider, helping asset managers across the globe build and grow their product suites. With an industry-leading index platform, it partners with issuers to develop innovative investment solutions and bring them to market. Beyond that, its modern distribution solutions help clients scale products and achieve success.

For more information, please visit: www.VettaFi.com.

About HANetf: HANetf is Europe's largest provider of white-label ETFs and ETC/Ps, providing asset managers across the world with a full white-label operational, regulatory, distribution and marketing solution to successfully launch and manage UCITS ETF and ETC/Ps. For investors, HANetf offers products geared to benefit from themes shaping the future. From the future of energy and drive for sustainability, to the future of defence, technology, recycled gold, cryptocurrencies, active strategies, and many more.

Media Contact: help@vettafi.com

VettaFi LLC, is a wholly-owned subsidiary of TMX Group Limited (TMX Group). For more information about TMX Group, please visit: www.tmx.com.

SOURCE: VettaFi

To view the source version of this press release, please visit https://www.newsfilecorp.com/release/260839
